concerns.text: Task T028 implements a Mixed-Effects Model (MixedLM) with a random effect (1|Subject), but the plan.md and spec.md (FR-004) explicitly define the model as a mass-univariate OLS regression. The task introduces a dependency on a statistical architecture (MixedLM) not authorized in the upstream plan, creating a semantic ordering violation where the implementation diverges from the design artifact it consumes.

Task T033 (Sensitivity Driver) re-runs the full statistical pipeline (T028-T032) 9 times. The plan.md timeline estimates ~4 hours total runtime based on a single model fit. T033 creates a hidden dependency on a resource budget (9x runtime) that the plan explicitly fails to account for, likely causing the pipeline to exceed the 6-hour CI limit (SC-001) before the task can complete. The task order assumes feasibility without the necessary resource allocation tasks.

Task T021 (parcel-wise processing loop) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, the task description states it handles 'one parcel at a time to stay under a moderate RAM footprint'. If T021 is executed in parallel with other tasks (e.g., T020 or T022) or if multiple instances of T021 are launched, the 'one parcel at a time' constraint implies a shared state or sequential resource lock that contradicts the [P] tag. The task is not parallel-safe as described.

Task T034 (Robustness check) re-runs the full pipeline on a low-motion subset. While the Constitution (Principle VI) mandates robustness checks, the spec.md User Story 3 only explicitly requires the *exclusion* of high-motion subjects (FR-002). T034 appears to be a scope-creep task not strictly ordered by the explicit FRs in the spec, though it aligns with the Constitution. It should be explicitly linked to Constitution Principle VI in the task description to justify its ordering.
